narration: Every time that tip screen spins around, you're being played. Americans tip $1,040 a year now — that's up 53% since 2019. And most of it isn't even your decision.
visual: Animated tablet tip screen springs in from center, dollar counter climbs from $0 to $1,040, dark background with orange accent

narration: Number one: the default button trap. They show you 25%, 30%, 35% — never 15%. Studies prove that defaults alone boost tips by 23%, before you even think about it.
visual: Tip screen showing three buttons: 25%, 30% pre-selected in orange, 35%, with a No Tip option tucked away at bottom. Light background.

narration: Number two: the pause of shame. The barista's watching, the line's behind you — that 8-second window is pure engineered pressure. It costs you $360 a year in tips you'd never choose alone.
visual: Person silhouette at counter, three people silhouettes in line behind, countdown timer arc in orange, $360/year stat fades in at bottom. Dark background.

narration: Number three: tip creep. iPad screens started at coffee shops. Now they're at hotel kiosks, airports, even self-checkout machines — places with zero human service asking for 20%.
visual: Four icons spring in with staggered animation: coffee cup, self-checkout kiosk, hotel building, airplane. Light background.

narration: Here's the actual math. Lunch, coffee, delivery, and dinner tips add up fast. Run the numbers across a year and you hit $1,040 — more than most people spend on a vacation.
visual: Itemized breakdown building line by line: lunch +$300, coffee +$260, delivery +$280, bars and dinner +$200, totaling $1,040. Dark background.

narration: The fix is dead simple. Decide your rules before the screen spins: 15% for counter service, 20% for sit-down, zero for self-checkout. That one decision saves you over a thousand dollars. Nobody's actually watching.
visual: Phone SVG showing three rule buttons, self-checkout $0 highlighted in green, SAVE $1,040/YEAR callout springs in. Light background.
